policy.title: RBI finalizes mandatory climate stress testing for scheduled commercial banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines requiring all scheduled commercial banks to conduct annual climate stress tests starting FY2027, with specific scenarios for physical risks (floods, cyclones, drought) and transition risks. Banks must integrate climate-adjusted PD/LGD into ICAAP submissions by March 2027.

policy.title: SEBI extends BRSR Core to top 500 listed companies with assurance mandate
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting with limited assurance for top 500 companies by market cap effective FY2026-27, expanding from the current top 150. Scope 3 emissions disclosure becomes mandatory for top 250 companies.

policy.title: IRDAI releases sandbox framework for parametric climate insurance products
policy.description: IRDAI published guidelines enabling insurers to pilot parametric products for agriculture and MSME segments under a 24-month regulatory sandbox. Products must use government-approved weather data sources and define trigger thresholds transparently.

policy.title: Basel Committee issues final standard on Pillar 3 climate-related disclosures
policy.description: BCBS finalized disclosure requirements for banks' climate risk exposures across credit, market, and operational risk, with phased implementation from January 2027. The framework mandates granular sectoral breakdowns for high-carbon portfolios.

policy.title: EU confirms CSRD applicability for non-EU companies with €150M+ EU revenue
policy.description: European Commission confirmed that non-EU parent companies with over €150 million EU revenue must report under CSRD from FY2028. Indian conglomerates with significant EU operations now face mandatory double materiality assessments.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating acute physical risk cascades and tipping point dynamics, recommending banks model non-linear agricultural and real estate losses. New scenarios include 2°C and 3°C chronic stress trajectories to 2060.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with SBI General Insurance for India climate risk expansion
competitors.description: Jupiter announced strategic partnership with SBI General to provide physical risk analytics for agricultural and property underwriting across India. The deal includes localized hazard models for monsoon variability and cyclone risk.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $12M Series A to expand climate risk analytics for Indian banks
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih closed Series A led by Nexus Venture Partners to build climate-adjusted credit risk modules targeting NBFC and mid-tier bank segments. The company claims 35 financial institution clients.

competitors.title: Moody's launches India-specific climate risk scores for bank loan portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's released physical and transition risk scores covering 15,000+ Indian corporate borrowers, integrated into its CreditView platform. Scores incorporate district-level flood and heat stress data mapped to borrower locations.

competitors.title: Watershed partners with HDFC Bank for Scope 3 financed emissions tracking
competitors.description: Watershed announced engagement with HDFC Bank to measure and track financed emissions across commercial lending portfolios. The pilot covers power, steel, and cement sectors representing 40% of HDFC's corporate book.

competitors.title: XDI releases asset-level physical risk API for emerging market infrastructure
competitors.description: XDI launched API product covering physical risk scores for 50 million infrastructure assets across South Asia, Southeast Asia, and Africa. Pricing targets development finance institutions and commercial banks at $0.02 per asset query.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory appointed by SIDBI for MSME climate risk assessment framework
competitors.description: GIST Advisory won mandate from SIDBI to develop climate vulnerability scoring methodology for MSME lending, covering 200,000+ borrowers. Framework will integrate into SIDBI's digital lending platform by Q3 2026.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Lending: Evidence from Emerging Markets
research.description: IFC study analyzing 850 EM banks finds that climate-exposed loan portfolios show 40-60 bps higher NPAs during extreme weather years. Banks with formal climate risk frameworks demonstrate 25% lower portfolio volatility.

research.title: Parametric Solutions for Climate Resilience in South Asia
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's parametric insurance protection gap at $15 billion annually for agriculture and SME segments. Report identifies rainfall index and cyclone wind-speed triggers as most viable product structures.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Financial Stability
research.description: BIS working paper finds that 70% of EM corporate borrowers lack standardized ESG disclosures, creating systemic blind spots for international lenders. Paper recommends proxy-based estimation methodologies using satellite and transaction data.

research.title: Physical Risk Transmission to Indian Bank Balance Sheets
research.description: RBI Occasional Paper models physical risk impact on Indian bank portfolios, estimating potential credit losses of 2.8-4.5% of agricultural and MSME exposures under severe flood scenarios. Study covers 12 public sector banks.

research.title: Index-Based Insurance for Climate Adaptation: Global Lessons
research.description: World Bank report reviews 45 parametric insurance programs globally, finding that basis risk remains primary adoption barrier. Programs using hyper-local weather stations show 30% higher farmer satisfaction than satellite-only triggers.

research.title: Integrating Climate Risk into Credit Ratings: A Framework for Banks
research.description: UNEP FI framework provides methodology for adjusting PD models with physical and transition risk factors, with case studies from 8 pilot banks. Recommends 5-20% PD uplift for high-carbon sectors under 2030 transition scenarios.

ourRead.headline: RBI's stress testing mandate creates an 18-month window for StepChange to become the default climate risk infrastructure for Indian banks.
body: This week's signals converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are moving from guidance to mandates, and global players are racing to fill the resulting demand. The RBI's final climate stress testing guidelines (policy-1) set a hard March 2027 deadline that will force every scheduled commercial bank to operationalize physical risk analytics—most have no internal capability to do this today.

The RBI mandate is StepChange's clearest near-term catalyst. Banks must now model climate-adjusted PD/LGD for ICAAP submissions, exactly matching our core product. SEBI's BRSR Core expansion (policy-2) creates parallel pressure on banks' corporate clients to disclose, which will drive demand for our ESG data layer. The policy window is narrow: banks will lock in vendors within 12 months.

The competitive signals are concerning but manageable. Jupiter's SBI General partnership (comp-1) validates the India opportunity but targets insurance, not banking credit risk. Sprih's $12M raise (comp-2) is the most direct threat—they're explicitly targeting NBFCs with climate-adjusted credit modules. Moody's India scores (comp-3) show global incumbents localizing fast. StepChange's edge is depth: proprietary hazard data at district level, not generic country scores. We must accelerate bank pilots before Sprih scales.

The research pipeline provides both ammunition and methodology. IFC's finding that climate-exposed portfolios show 40-60 bps higher NPAs (research-1) is the business case stat every bank CFO needs. RBI's own paper modeling 2.8-4.5% potential losses (research-4) legitimizes our approach. UNEP FI's PD adjustment framework (research-6) should be integrated into our methodology documentation immediately—banks will want alignment with recognized standards.

One thing to watch: IRDAI's parametric sandbox (policy-3) opens a second revenue stream, but requires government-approved weather data sources—we need to confirm our data inputs qualify. One action this week: reach out to RBI-regulated banks in the 10-50 priority list with a compliance-focused pitch deck citing the March 2027 deadline and the RBI Occasional Paper findings.
